note: Measured longitude based on the source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

Preliminary measurement following the brightest leading edge portions seen to the north and to the southeast. The measured parameters were based on the source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available and the measured parameters may vary as a result.
Measurement based on source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the potential source location. The CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 imagery, and leading to additional uncertainty about the accuracy of the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.
Measurement based on the potential source location using STEREO A COR2 imagery only.

Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and faint frames of the front in SOHO LASCO C2. CME half width is less than 10 degrees, perhaps around 5 degrees, limited by the 10 degree half-width minimum in swpc_cat.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
Measurement based on source location near the NNE limb.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement relies heavily on later frames in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as there appears to be some acceleration as the CME propagates farther from the Sun.
Measurement based on probable source location on the SW limb around W90.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible source location near NE limb.
Updated measurement based on STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 which have the clearest view of the separate features between CME: 2026-03-20T12:23Z and CME: 2026-03-20T12:38Z.
Preliminary analysis based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Very approximate, somewhat speculative analysis based on the fitting of the fuzzy faint front in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Very speculative analysis using triangulation and based on the fitting of two very faint fronts in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.
3D analysis with one perspective, using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, as STEREO A COR2 does not observe the CME front.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery of the possible shock front of this CME. It is unclear if this is a shock or a fainter CME following the same path ahead of the brighter leading edge measurement.

Parameters based on best fit of brighter portion of CME as seen in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty with this CME as to whether this is a separate event or associated with the same eruption.
Final analysis of this CME with later imagery from SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and with a few early timestamps of STEREO A COR2 before the routine evening data gap.

Analysis of the fainter wider shock front based on the fit with the imagery from SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, including the early imagery from STEREO A COR2 before the usual evening data gap and fully available SOHO LASCO imagery.

Triangulated bulk analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Due to the distorted lobed shape of the CME front, this analysis best captures the faster northern leading edge of the CME front using the full width of the bright bulk material. The CME front is further distorted in STEREO A COR2's field-of-view, but able to be captured good enough to track with the other coronagraphs.

SHOCK analysis using only SOHO LASCO C3, as the shock front is not resolvable in STEREO A COR2 imagery potentially due to faintness, being directed towards the spacecraft, and/or the COR2A data gap ending at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The longitude could vary up to W55-W85, the source longitude of W65 is used as a best approximation with available imagery.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using later frames only due to observed acceleration when including early frames. The CME shape is very distorted in STEREO A COR2 compared to SOHO LASCO, appearing much wider. Using STEREO A COR2's width, the leading edge still tracks very well between COR2 and C3.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Updated measurement with additional coronagraph imagery. Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate based on location near the east limb.
Updated parameters based on SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with matching to possible features in STEREO A COR2 following the data gap. Possible deflection within the FOV due to nearby CH.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Updated measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, as the CME accelerates after LASCO C2 visibility. The CME front is notably wider in LASCO C3 than STEREO A COR2, so the LASCO C3 width is used.

Single spacecraft 3D analysis using W100 as an estimation of the source location of former AR 14392 which has rotated around the SW limb, as such the longitude could vary W95-W105. CME half width is barely 10 degrees, perhaps a little smaller.

Bulk analysis of the bright, inner material of this front observed best in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, which dissipates/blends into the background after its earliest frames. Triangulation puts it about 9 degrees west of the observed source, at E51 with observed E60, but the wide field line movement and EUV wave support this deflection.

Preliminary shock analysis, which may be considered final pending further review, using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as the shock front is not observed in STEREO A COR2 for triangulation. As such, the observed source region at E60 is used as a reference and the actual longitude may vary E50-E70. This is a wide analysis including a southern part of the feature believed to be of this same shock front and not a separate event, as the entire feature travels at the same speed through the coronagraphs.
Updated measurement of the leading edge with SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A.
Leading edge of the jet with uncertainty in longitude approximately 10 degrees.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the source location. Since this event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 and a triangulated measurement is therefore not possible, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery as well as the source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as visible source candidate.
Very speculative analysis based on the location of a possible but not definitive source (eruption in AR 14403) and on the front in one coronagraph (SOHO LASCO).

Approximate analysis based on the fit of this very faint front in all three coronagraphs, 
SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. No source has been found in the GOES SUVI EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of this very faint, narrow front in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No clear source has been found for this very faint minor CME in EUV imagery, however there is a very minor dimming at ~(S35E55) seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery, tentatively confirming the analysis based on the fit of the front of the CME in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A coronagraphs.

Final analysis of the brighter bulk of this CME based on the location of the X-class flare and associated eruption, made with fully available coronagraph imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and a couple of GOES CCOR-1 images.
A very speculative analysis of the faint shock of this CME using mostly the SOHO LASCO imagery since there was no good fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2).
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 in later frames.

Parameters based on potential source location and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 imagery. The front of this CME is diffuse and may cause some uncertainty with parameters. This CME is not clearly seen in other coronagraphs due to various reasons (see main CME note).
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Parameters based on source and adjusted for best fit of visible features in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Deflection is visible in the first few initial frames in SOHO LASCO C2 to the NW. This is likely due to the proximity of a coronal hole.
3D SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis using W12 as the source longitude. CME front dissipates after 3 visible frames.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with available imagery of the faint front.

3D SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis, using the W11 source longitude, finding a further southern latitude than expected. With the filement ejecta poorly erupting and coronal hole proximity, deflection is likely, and the lack of a visible front in STEREO A COR2 means no triangulation possible.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated analysis between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Half width visibly <10 degrees, approximately 5 degrees or less.
Updated measurement with additional available SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on the source location centered around longitude W60, but the filament eruption extends across at least 40 degrees, based on SUVI imagery.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the source longitude.
Measurement based on the source longitude.
Measurement of the fast portion/leading edge of the CME which is most likely to be associated with the filament eruption seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.
Low confidence measurement of the CME using GOES and SOHO and the assumption that the CME is far-sided; Earth-sided fits were also able to be conducted for this CME with some poor skill.
Preliminary shock front measurement using only a few early available frames of GOES CCOR-1.
Updated bulk measurement with more GOES CCOR-1 frames out to solar 13 solar radii but still without SOHO imagery due to no downlinked imagery at the time of measurement.
Measurement based on the potential source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A imagery.
Preliminary leading edge measurement out to 13 solar radii using SOHO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Updated measurement using SOHO C2, C3 and GOES CCOR-1 which suggests the filament eruption CME is wider than previously thought--its width increases as it propagates outward into the field of view, especially in SOHO C2/C3.

Approximate analysis with additional imagery from LASCO and STEREO A COR2, based on the fit of the fuzzy faint front in two coronagraphs. There seems to be some eastward deflection from the dimming considered to be the source of this CME.
Measurement based on later frames of the CME in SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery, based on the best fit in SWPC_CAT.
Updated measurement with additional imagery. The leading edge of the CME fades away quickly in available running difference imagery.
Updated measurement based on the potential source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and one available frame of GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. Triangulates well around W105-W115.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found for this event in the EUV imagery, so this analysis cannot be confirmed by the location of source.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2.

STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 triangulation of the bright inner bulk. This bulk appears to have some concentric features, likely due to multiple observed snaps of field lines in SDO imagery, but all travel together and are analyzed as one front as a result.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Single coronagraph measurement based on visible source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

Speculative analysis based on the fit of a very faint front in STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2 coronagraphs, assuming that it is the same CME event. No clear source has been found for this CME. The half-width is based on the width of a very faint front in SOHO LASCO C2, while the CME appears much thinner in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the on-disk source location.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Parameters based on potential source area with a wide range of uncertainty due to limited stereoscopic viewpoints of source location and CME. These parameters assume the source began near Active Region 14405 (S28W152), however Active Region 14409 (N01W140) also provides a fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with speeds closer to 500 km/s.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1
Single spacecraft 3D analysis with GOES CCOR-1 with no STEREO A COR2 to triangulate, using the observed source centered at N19E51.
Parameters based on potential source location area and visual of what may be the CME in STEREO A COR2 to triangulate, however STEREO A COR2 imagery was not included in this measurement.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1, and a few SOHO LASCO C3 frames following a data gap.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO, STEREO A, and GOES CCOR-1.
Updated, triangulated measurement based on additional imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and on visible possible source location.
Triangulation between the faint feature in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation, tracking the front direction with a bit of discrepancy on the width between the two coronagraphs (COR2A's view is used for the width).
GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.
GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.
Speculative measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. There is a higher than normal degree of uncertainty in this measurement due to poor image quality.
Measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source location.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Parameters based on potential source location and best fit using STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event is partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1.
STEREO A only measurement based on source location and best fit, as CME is difficult to distinguish from earlier CME: 2026-04-21T00:00Z in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Leading edge measurement using only GOES and SOHO imagery as there was a STEREO A data gap at the time of analysis.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO, GOES, and STEREO A coronagraphs to ascertain the longitude; there is clear deflection to the north based on the measurement.

Leading edge of bulk of the left-most edge (in the ecliptic plane) of the CME bulk which is fairly asymmetric; the portion out of the ecliptic generally moves slower relative to the portion in the ecliptic.
Measurement of the more symmetric shock front which much better fitting using triangulation relative to the asymmetric bulk.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between al three coronagraphs to ascertain the longitude.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Approximate preliminary analysis based on the fit with two available coronagraphs (GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2) in the absence of SOHO LASCO data due to scheduled temporary data gap. There seems to be NW deflection from the source (AR 14419 at N18W65).

SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis at E90, originally as a plane-of-sky, but is a close approximation to the CME limb source observed in SDO to consider a valid 3D measurement. Half width is less than 10 degrees.

Approximate preliminary analysis with GOES CCOR-1 and early STEREO A COR2 imagery (before STEREO A night time daily data gap). There seems to be a NW deflection from the source location of Active Region 14419 (N15W67).
Single spacecraft 3D analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The source longitude could vary E80-E100, so an approximation of E90 is used for analysis.
Analysis of the brighter bulk front of the CME based on the fit with all three coronagraphs, including STEREO A COR2. Analysis suggests a significant eastwardly deflection.
Approximate analysis of the fainter, wider shock front of this CME based on the fit with all three coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit of the brighter bulk front with three coronagraphs.
Analysis of the faint partial halo shock front with LASCO and GOES CCOR-1, while the shock in STEREO A COR2 does not produce the fit with these other instruments.

Analysis based on the fit of the faint front in two coronagraphs, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. There seems to be an eastward deflection from the source, likely due to the small coronal hole to the SW of the source.
Updated one coronagraph measurement with more GOES CCOR-1 frames with high uncertainty in longitude and whether there is any deflection.
